entry.title: SAP S/4HANA 2025: Key Innovations in Sales for the Cloud Private Edition
entry.content:
🌟 SAP S/4HANA 2025 Key Innovations in Sales for cloud Private Edition

With the October 2025 release of SAP S/4HANA 2025 Cloud Private Edition, SAP continues to strengthen the Sales line of business through new capabilities that improve performance, automation, and process transparency.
After exploring the latest updates, I’ve summarized some of the highlights that deliver the most value—spanning Output Management, extensibility, order processing, returns management, and more.

🔎How to Explore What’s New in SAP S/4HANA Releases

If you want to compare innovations between releases—for example, from S/4HANA 2023 → S/4HANA 2025—the SAP Help Portal and the What's New Viewer remain the most efficient way to explore updates by product, line of business, and topic.

👉You can access the What's New Viewer What's New Viewer - SAP S/4HANA and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition directly from the SAP Help Portal and filter by:

  • Product: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Line of Business: Sales
  • Type: New or Changed
  • Version range: From your current release to a target release

This allows you to quickly identify innovations relevant to your business processes.

Key Sales Innovations in SAP S/4HANA 2025

☘️ Output Management Enhancements (NAST + Output Control)

One of the major improvements in S/4HANA 2025 is SAP’s expanded support for Output Control in combination with legacy NAST‑generated forms.

Preview NAST Forms in Fiori Apps

If you previously used NAST forms and later activated Output Management, you can now preview these legacy forms directly in Fiori, including:

👉  Manage Billing Documents: Document Preview (F0797)

👉  Manage Sales Orders Version 2: Document Preview (F3893) 

No additional configuration is required—your existing NAST setup simply needs to remain active.

Here an example:

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide->Cross-Application Components->Output Control->Manage Application Object Type Activation

In Manage Sales Order – Version 2 ( F3693 ) with a sales order that the form was generated by NAST:

You can preview the form: 

💡Business Value: More flexibility to Customer outputs forms.

 

☘️ New Flexibility for Global Trade Management

In Trading Contracts, you can now choose between:

👉NAST

👉SAP S/4HANA Output Control 

👉Or both, at the contract type level

This gives organizations more flexibility during transitions between output frameworks.

New Output Management for Global Trade Management

By default, the message determination for trading contracts is still NAST so if you decide to use Output Control you should configure first the object type activation for:

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide->Cross-Application Components->Output Control->Manage Application Object Type Activation

And then you can choose by Trading Contract type which Output Control indicator prefer:

SAP Customizing Impl. Guide->Logistics – General->Global Trade Management->Trading Contract->Define Trading Contract Types

💡Business Value: More features for Trading Contracts.

 

☘️ Import Sales Orders: Reference-Based Creation

A highly valuable enhancement: You can now create sales orders with reference to quotations or contracts using the Import Sales Orders app 

The imported spreadsheet now supports:

👉Header reference data

👉Item reference data

👉Full or partial carry-over of preceding document information

The new template include:

💡Business Value: This reduces manual effort, improves data consistency, and accelerates end-to-end sales order creation.

 

☘️ New Extensibility: Sales Order API (A2X, OData V4)

SAP delivers a modernized and more efficient API Sales Order (A2X, OData V4) |built on the ABAP RESTful Application Programming Model (RAP). It offers:

👉Faster processing performance

👉Asynchronous execution

👉Ability to return minimal or full responses

👉Full access to pricing details, including subtotals

👉Enhanced filtering for sub-entities (items, partners, values, etc.)

This empowers developers to create more scalable and performant integrations.

To explore available APIs please follow this link: SAP Business Accelerator Hub

💡Business Value: Improves process performance

 

☘️ New CDS Views for Global Trade Management

S/4HANA 2025 introduces additional CDS Views for GTM supporting:

👉Customer and supplier confirmation emails

👉Header-level contract information

👉Output parameter determination

👉Value helps (organizational data, user IDs, contract types, etc.)

💡Business Value: These new views help streamline reporting, automation, and custom extensions. For more information, please see CDS Views for Global Trade Management | SAP Help Portal

 

☘️ Claims, Returns, and Refund Management

I’ve explored into Process Navigator - SAP for Me  into SAP Best Practices for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and the Customer Returns (BKP) process receives important updates in S/4Hana 2025.

🙌  Determine No Refund for Logistical Follow-Up Activity Receive into Plant

You have the option not to refund the customer when you select the logistical follow-up activity Receive into Plant (0001) in the returns order and later on  you can still create a credit memo request or a delivery free-of-charge with reference to the relevant returns order item.

🙌 Automatic Inspection After Goods Receipt in Customer Returns

You can enter the inspection result when you create a returns order, before the product is returned. If you already know the inspection result before you receive the product and don’t want to go through another inspection after it has been returned, you can ask the system to automatically create and complete the inspection using the inspection data and the next follow-up activity you provide when you create the returns order. As a result, after you receive the product, the system completes the inspection and triggers the specified subsequent logistic steps automatically.

🙌Refurbishment Using Maintenance Order as Follow-Up Activity

A maintenance order is created for returned products to initiate the refurbishment process. The maintenance order is configured as a refurbishment order.

🙌Refurbishment Using Production Order as Follow-Up Activity

A production order is created for returned products to initiate the refurbishment process. The production version specified for the production order must be a specific version that indicates refurbishment.

🙌Quality Notification as a Follow-Up Document

When saving a returns order, you can create a quality notification in quality management to trigger a parallel quality management process. The necessary settings for output management using output determination (SD-BF-OC) to create the quality notifications are already maintained.

🙌Valuation with Reference to Preceding Sales Process

Traditionally, return deliveries are valuated based on current material valuation.
Now, S/4HANA 2025 allows you to reuse the valuation of the original outbound delivery, which is critical when:

👉Sales context changed over time

👉Historical valuation is required

👉Margin or profitability analysis must stay accurate

This is enabled via a customizing switch and applied at the document level.

📌Note: The checkbox into the Customer Return item level is currently not available in Fiori apps like Manage Customer Returns or Version 2.  It is accessible through the Create Sales Document transaction in SAP GUI, but don’t worry is planned to include into “Manage Customer Returns Version2” in the future.

💡Business Value: It streamlines the return process, resulting in better customer service, while also completing the product's journey internally or forwarding them to the supplier if necessary.

 

☘️ Joule: AI-Driven Sales Insights

SAP continues expanding Joule’s embedded AI capabilities. For Sales, new features include:

👉Faster insights into the order fulfillment cycle

👉Assistance with delivery and supply issue resolution

👉Guided billing document creation

👉Support to streamline the solution quotation process

💡Business Value: These capabilities help both sales and customer service teams act faster and with more context.

For Joule capabilities in detail, you can visit: Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition | SAP Help Portal

🎯Final Thoughts

SAP S/4HANA 2025 brings meaningful advancements to the Sales line of business by strengthening output flexibility, enhancing extensibility through RAP-based APIs, improving efficiency in order creation, and providing smarter returns process.
Combined with Joule’s growing AI insights, organizations gain more automation, transparency, and decision support than ever before.

 

I hope this was helpful for you 😊

I'm looking forward to your feedback and thoughts. You can post your questions in the SAP community, referring to this blog post.

Thank you,

Brought to you by the SAP S/4HANA RIG team.

entry.title: Release Navigator for SAP Cloud ERP Private - What's New in 2025 FPS01
entry.content:
Happy 2026 SAP Universe!

Welcome to a new year. We’re excited to kick things off with the upcoming SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025 FPS01 release, arriving this February. This update brings a rich set of new features and enhancements across the SAP Cloud ERP suite—furthering our commitment to helping you run your business with more efficiency, intelligence, and compliance.

As with every new release, there is a wealth of fresh information, documentation, and enablement resources to explore. The good news? The release navigator for SAP Cloud ERP Private is already in the process of updating all content to reflect the 2025 FPS01 release. This update will be fully completed during the week of February 27th, and no action is required on your part. SAP will handle everything for you.

 

Access the release navigator for SAP Cloud ERP Private

 

Tool Overview

The release navigator for SAP Cloud ERP Private, delivered through SAP Enterprise Support, is an essential resource for end users and administrators alike. It simplifies how you explore and manage new release content by providing:

  • Curated access to relevant documentation and tools
  • Guided insights tailored to your functional needs
  • Enablement opportunities, including webinars, workshops, and training, designed to help you make the most of each release

Whether you're responsible for system administration or simply want to understand what’s new, the release navigator is your one-stop destination for staying informed.

 

Content Insights

To help you quickly find what matters most, here’s a snapshot of the new and continuously updated content aligned with the 2025 FPS01 release. If you have suggestions for future topics, we’d love to hear from you in the comments.

entry.title: How AI Automation Eliminates Master Data Duplicates in SAP
entry.content:
The Business Case

Whether running SAP ECC or S/4HANA, most organizations struggle with duplicate master data for materials, vendors, and customers. This isn't just a "clutter" problem, poor material master data leads to users creating Purchase Requisitions with text items, bypassing procurement controls and ruining spend analytics.

Let’s explore how AI automation and data vectorization can clean up the core and prevent duplicates before they happen.

The Engine: What is Data Vectorization?

At Skybuffer, we are constantly exploring the benefits of SAP data vectorization. Simply put, vectorization converts data into numerical arrays (vectors) using embedding functions.

Unlike standard SAP keyword searches, vectors are language-independent and allow algorithms to perform similarity searches. While standard tables see rows and columns, a vectorized model sees the relationship between data points.

How Does It Help?

When data is vectorized, we can execute hybrid searches that are indifferent to word order or exact spelling.

Example: Imagine searching for a global customer like "Skybuffer." You might have different accounts for Poland and Norway. If a user tries to create a new record, they can simply ask:

"Do we have a Customer master record for Skybuffer in Poland?"

By querying vectorized KNA1 table data, the AI identifies the record even if the naming convention differs slightly.

 Pic.1 Hybrid Search based on Vectorized KNA1 Table Data

Real-World Integration Points

Hybrid search is most powerful when embedded directly into the SAP business process:

  • Preventative Checks: During the "Save" action in SAP, a custom check against vectorized data can alert the user to a potential duplicate.

  • External Access: Quick searches via Microsoft Teams or SAP Fiori to find existing masters on the go.

  • Smart Procurement: Identifying an existing Material Master during the ordering process, preventing the user from resorting to a "text item."

 Pic.2 Quick Searches of SAP Master Data based on Vectorized Data

Conclusion

Generative AI is a powerhouse when used wisely. Many companies struggle to find the right use cases, often just mirroring existing SAP processes in a chat window. That isn’t true AI automation.

Our approach is different: we focus on the gaps in standard automation. By using AI to simplify data discovery, we reduce development effort and provide an intelligent, frictionless experience for SAP users.

Closing Statement

Keep automating. Remember: smart customization is a competitive advantage, not a deviation from the standard. Build with AI in mind to slash support costs and maximize your ROI via the Skybuffer AI platform.

entry.title: Build a Winning IT Operating Model for Your S/4HANA Investment
entry.content:
Werde zum Value Partner: Entfessele das volle Potenzial der S/4HANA-Investition mit dem richtigen IT Operating Model!

Die digitale Transformation ist immer noch für viele Unternehmen ein zentrales strategisches Ziel – und SAP S/4HANA spielt dabei in vielen Unternehmen eine Schlüsselrolle. Ein maßgeschneidertes IT Operating Model (ITOM) ist die Voraussetzung dafür, dass die Potenziale von S/4HANA voll ausgeschöpft werden können. Dieser Beitrag zeigt, worauf es beim Aufbau eines ITOM im SAP-Kontext ankommt – und welche strategischen Entscheidungen dabei zu treffen sind.​ 

Ausgangslage 

Ein wirksames ITOM entsteht nicht im luftleeren Raum. Vielmehr hängt es entscheidend vom Reifegrad der bestehenden SAP-Landschaft und Organisation ab:​ 

  • SAP bereits umfänglich im Einsatz: Wenn SAP bereits tief in der Organisation verankert ist, existieren in der Regel klare Verantwortlichkeiten, Prozesse und ein grundlegendes Verständnis für die SAP-Anwendungen und Technologien.​ 
  • SAP als Nischenlösung: Wird SAP nur in einzelnen Bereichen z.B. in der Buchhaltung eingesetzt, sind die Erfahrungen mit SAP zu arbeiten mit etwas Geschick auch auf andere Bereiche z.B. Produktionsplanung, Logistik übertragbar. Schließlich weiß die Organisation bereits, wie SAP "tickt". 
  • SAP als Neuland: Hier muss sich erst ein breites Verständnis für die SAP-Anwendungen und Technologien entwickeln im Projektverlauf und nach dem go-live im Betrieb. In Unternehmen ohne SAP-Vorerfahrung ist zusätzlich in Change-Management, Qualifizierungsmaßnahmen und kulturelle Integration zu investieren.​ 

Je nach Ausgangssituation müssen IT-Verantwortliche ihr ITOM gezielt ausrichten – sowohl organisatorisch als auch kulturell. Der enge Schulterschluss mit den Fachbereichen ist essenziell. In allen drei Ausgangssituationen hat die S/4 HANA-Einführung eine Vielzahl von Implikationen z.B. auf die Governance,  auf die Skalierbarkeit, auf den Einsatz neuer Technologien, Arbeitsweisen und Prozesse.​ 

Kritische Erfolgsdimensionen für das ITOM​ 

Beim Design des Operating Models müssen verschiedene Schlüsselbereiche berücksichtigt werden. Viele Darstellungen beschränken sich auf die 3 Dimensionen Prozesse, Technologie und Mensch. Erfahrungsgemäß lohnt sich eine genauere Aufteilung der Schlüsselbereiche 

  • Architektur: Häufig bereits zu Projektbeginn definiert, bildet sie eine feste Rahmenbedingung – sollte aber regelmäßig auf strategische Passfähigkeit geprüft werden.​ 
  • Tooling & IT-Prozesse: Von DevOps über Monitoring bis hin zu Support-Prozessen – effiziente Toolchain und klar definierte Prozesse z.B. nach ITIL sind unerlässlich.​ 
  • Organisationsstruktur: Ob zentralisiert, dezentral oder hybrid – die Struktur muss zur operativen Realität passen.​ 
  • Prozessansatz: Funktionale oder End-to-End-Ausrichtung oder beides? Der gewählte Ansatz hat direkte Auswirkungen auf Verantwortlichkeiten und Schnittstellen.​ 
  • Way of Working: Agile, klassische oder hybride Arbeitsweisen beeinflussen das Design der Rollen und Aufgabenverteilung.​ 
  • Change Management, Ausbildung, Kompetenz: Mit Unsicherheiten und Skepsis in der Belegschaft sollte offensiv umgegangen werden. Eine fokussierte Change-Begleitung ist sehr zu empfehlen für den Gesamterfolg der Transformation. 

Nicht alle dieser Bereiche sind frei gestaltbar – viele sind durch bestehende IT-Strategien oder Unternehmensvorgaben determiniert. Daher ist es essenziell, zu Beginn des ITOM-Designs zwischen fixen Rahmenbedingungen und flexibel gestaltbaren Elementen zu unterscheiden. Mit einer S/4HANA Transformation besteht die unbedingte Möglichkeit, Strukturen zu hinterfragen und "alte Zöpfe" neu zu ordnen.​ 

Wie sich die verschiedenen Schlüsselbereiche in den Projekt- und Betriebskontext einordnen lassen und welche Aufgaben der/die ITOM-Verantwortliche währende die S/4 HANA Einführung hat, zeigt die folgende Tabelle in einer Übersicht. 

 Schlüsselbereiche für den CCOE Aufbau

Strukturierter Aufbau: Vorgehensmodell zur ITOM-Entwicklung​ 

Folgende Schritte haben sich in der Praxis bewährt:​ 

1. Benennung eines verantwortlichen ITOM Leads:  

  • Sicherstellung klarer Verantwortlichkeiten für Design und Umsetzung des Operating Models. 
  • Verankerung der Rolle auf ausreichender Hierarchieebene (z. B. direkt unterhalb der CIO-/IT-Leitung).​ 
  • Aufbau eines schlagkräftigen Kernteams mit Vertretern aus relevanten Domänen (z. B. Architektur, Betrieb, Prozesse).​ 

2. Definition von Zielbild, Prioritäten und Vision​ 

  • Erarbeitung eines klaren Zielbilds für das zukünftige ITOM: Rolle, Beitrag zur Gesamtstrategie, Abgrenzung.​ 
  • Festlegung von Leitzielen wie z. B. Skalierbarkeit, Automatisierung oder Business-Alignment.​ 
  • Identifikation und Priorisierung der größten Hebel und Handlungsfelder – z.B. auch die Etablierung neuer “Spielregeln” wie Clean Core, SAP BTP first bei Erweiterungen, durchgängige Tool Chain 

(3. Optional) Bestandsaufnahme des aktuellen TOM (As-Is)​ 

  • Erhebung vorhandener Rollen, Prozesse, Tools und Strukturen (ggf. durch Interviews, Workshops, Dokumentenanalyse).​
  • Bewertung der Reifegrade in den einzelnen ITOM-Dimensionen.​ 
  • Identifikation von Redundanzen, Lücken, Pain Points oder auch Best Practices im aktuellen Setup.​ 

4. Definition zentraler Aufgabenbereiche (Building Blocks siehe Grafik)​ 

  • Clusterung aller ITOM-Aufgabenbereiche nach strategischen, Governance- und operativen Fähigkeiten - z.B. Sourcingstrategie, Business- und IT-Zusammenarbeit, Applikations- und SAP-Basis Betrieb 
  • Orientierung an IT-Prozessen und –Funktionen sowie der Business- und IT Zusammenarbeit. 
  • Klare Abgrenzung der Aufgabenbereiche zur Vermeidung von Verantwortlichkeitslücken.​ 

Aufgabenbereiche im S/4 HANA Betrieb

5. Erste Zuordnung von Rollen zu Aufgabenbereichen (High-Level)​ 

  • Benennung von Rollen pro Aufgabenbereich – z. B. Produkt Owner, Architekt, Applikations-/Prozess-Experte.​ 
  • Berücksichtigung vorhandener Rollen in der Organisation zur Vermeidung von Doppelstrukturen.​ 
  • Differenzierung zwischen Rollen und Stellen. 

6. Detaillierung von Rollen und Verantwortlichkeiten​ 

  • Detaillierung der Aufgabenbereiche und Erstellung eines RACI-Modells zur klaren Aufgabenverteilung (Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed).​ 
  • Beschreibung der Rollenprofile inklusive benötigter Kompetenzen, KPIs und Zielbilder.​ 
  • Validierung der Rollenzuordnung mit Fachbereichen, Betriebs- und Projektorganisationen. 

7. Identifikation von Lernbedarfen und Kompetenzlücken​ 

  • Ableitung von Trainingspfaden für bestehende Mitarbeitende basierend auf neuem Rollen- und Skillbedarf.​ 
  • Aufbau von Lernformaten - z. B. learning journeys, E-Learnings, SAP Learning Hub.​ 
  • Identifikation von Bedarf an Neueinstellungen oder externem Know-how - z. B. in Architektur, DevOps. 

8. Definition der Zielorganisation und Teamstrukturen​ 

  • Entwicklung eines Organisationsdesigns inkl. Teamgrößen, Berichtslinien und Verantwortlichkeiten - z.B. was gehört in das SAP CCOE und was nicht.
  • Entscheidung über Betriebsmodelle - z. B. zentral vs. dezentral, Inhouse vs. Outsourcing. 
  • Verankerung der ITOM-Strukturen im Operating Model der Gesamt-IT.
  • Organisatorischer Umgang mit neuen Themen z.B. Künstliche Intelligenz 

Ein iterativer, faktenbasierter Ansatz stellt sicher, dass das Operating Model nicht nur auf dem Papier funktioniert, sondern im operativen Alltag tragfähig ist.​ 

​ ...

Fazit​ 

Ein zielgerichtetes Operating Model für SAP S/4HANA ist ein zentraler Erfolgsfaktor für die digitale Transformation der IT-Landschaft. Es ermöglicht nicht nur einen stabilen und skalierbaren Betrieb der SAP-Plattform, sondern schafft auch die Grundlage für Innovation und Agilität. IT-Verantwortliche sollten die Gestaltung des ITOM daher als strategische Aufgabe begreifen – mit klarer Vision, strukturierter Umsetzung und konsequenter Verankerung in der Organisation. 

 ...

Torsten Scheffler ist selbständiger Berater und hat über 200 CCOE Projekte durchgeführt. 

...

Useful links: 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-architecture-blog-posts/building-high-performing-sap-it-organization-the-art-of-organizational/ba-p/13464999

entry.title: Stop Building from Scratch: Autonomous AI Agents for SAP Sales Order Processing
entry.content:
Why this article?

It is great that we are living in the era of AI technologies where humans are still needed to design and automate 😀. But let’s be honest: give it a few more years and no company will ask "Which AI platform should we choose?" That decision will be made by central AI services based on strict country and corporate regulations. The only thing that will matter is security and reliability.

So, why am I writing this? Because I see so many people struggling with custom, built-from-scratch AI automation solutions at the enterprise level. These projects start quickly, but they get stuck just as fast.

You know, it is like cookies 😀. You can make one in a sandbox; however, to scale that to a corporate production level is a totally different problem.

If you are still wondering why this article matters: I want to highlight that while humans are still designing automation, speed is everything. Deploying autonomous AI agents on a solid, reliable platform is a massive competitive advantage. Those playing around with internal IT projects or relying on System Integrators (SI) to build from zero are being left behind.

Let me explain why using one simple example: Customer Purchase Orders.

The Business Case: The Reality of POs

A company wins a deal (congratulations!), and the Customer sends a Purchase Order (PO).

In an ideal world, this happens via EDI. If you have EDI, you are lucky and you can stop reading. But in the real world, customers send PDFs, scanned images with handwriting, Excel files with macros, or even a screenshot of their laptop screen.

This shouldn't be an issue. Innovative platforms must handle data extraction from unstructured sources out of the box.

“Training? Fine-tuning??”

I know some of you just thought that 😊. Yes, training OCR models used to be mandatory. If you haven't faced the pain of training models, be happy, because you are young enough 😊. Today, this must work without that hassle.

The Main Challenge: Master Data Mapping

Extracting text is easy. The real challenge is my favorite one: Master Data Mapping.

How do we robustly and reliably read the customer’s product description and map it to the correct Master Data Object in our SAP system? And how do we do it without heavy development?

The Solution: SAP Master Data Vectorization

We don't need custom code; we need Vectorization.

Generative AI can write SQL queries, sure. But think about it: how many SQL queries would the AI have to guess to find a material that is semantically similar but written differently? Infinite. Or maybe a few less if you are lucky and the description contains an acronym like "A4 Paper" 😊.

When AI looks for a material master in vectorized data (rather than a database table), it reads by sense, not just by keywords. This allows it to reliably find the right SAP object even if the customer's description is vague.

I know only one SAP-certified Business AI Platform that has this Vectorization feature in its standard set: Skybuffer AI.

 Pic.1 SAP Master Data Vectorization Setup

 Pic.2 Example of Material Master Similarities based Search

The Implementation: Autonomous Agents in Action

This is the essential part: stop playing in the sandbox. Take a platform and create a simple autonomous AI agent.

In this example, I am using Skybuffer AI because it handles the heavy lifting out of the box:

  • Vectorizes SAP master data for semantic search.

  • Reads Emails (including complex attachments and OCR).

  • Safety First: Uses Mailboxes as a staging area (no messy data replication).

  • ABAP Control: It doesn't let the AI "guess" how to create data. It passes structured JSON to an ABAP class/BAPI for safe execution.

  • No Code: configured entirely in SAP Fiori.

The Flow

A Skybuffer AI agent acts effectively as an automated employee. You can schedule it, give it tools, and let it run.

 Pic.3 Scheduling Autonomous AI Agent

Step 1: The Input

The Agent connects to a Mailbox. It reads the email, extracts the attachment data, and places it into the Agent's memory.

 Pic.4 Flow inside the Tool of Skybuffer AI Action Server

Step 2: The Brain (Generative AI)

We connect the Generative AI and give it guidelines.

Note: The vectorized SAP master data is mapped directly to this action.

 Pic.5 Generative AI Action of Skybuffer AI Action Server

The AI creates a JSON structure based on our request. Crucially, we do not ask the AI to update SAP directly. We hand that JSON over to an SAP ABAP class. This ensures the transactional data is created correctly, without hallucinations.

Step 3: The Output

We update the email in the Mailbox with the status and send a confirmation back to the customer.

Everything runs either in your SAP HANA On-Premise shell (directly in your network) or on your SAP BTP tenant.

Conclusion

Autonomous AI agents are no longer "future tech", they are easy solutions available now.

By choosing a Business AI automation platform rather than building from scratch, your company gets the best of Generative AI and the SAP backend immediately.

It is strange that so many companies are still "playing" with in-house development. You wouldn't build your own Sales Order Management system from scratch instead of using SAP, right? 😊 That would be weird. Yet, companies are giving huge budgets to internal teams to build AI wrappers.

You don't need a giant team. You need a reliable solution, speed, and flexibility. Just look at Cursor, they revolutionized coding with roughly 60 employees before their massive 900M funding round 😉.

Be quick. Be autonomous.

entry.title: Transport Mechanism of Key-User Extensibility Objects in Public Cloud & Private Cloud
entry.content:
We are now quite familiar with the concept of Key User Extensibility/In-App Extensibility while working in a S/4 HANA Cloud environment. Key User Extensibility is available in both the environments of S/4 HANA Cloud (Public Edition & Private Edition) and is the preferred mode of creating extension due to its highest Clean Core level and being lightweight.

There are many blogposts available in the community showing creation and usage of the most commonly used Key User Extensibility Objects like Custom Fields / Custom Business Objects / Custom CDS Views etc.

However, in this blog post we will discuss about the difference in approach to Transport the Key User Extensibility objects to higher environments (Dev -> Test/QA ->Prod) in S/4 HANA Cloud, Public Edition & S/4 HANA Cloud Private Edition .

Considering a use case of  Custom fields for demonstration. Although for creation and modification of custom fields, the approach is similar in both Private and Public cloud but there are few differences in approach while it comes to transporting them and one of the key differences being -

A transport process for Key User Extensibility objects in Private Cloud are  associated with an actual Transport Request number while in Public Cloud there is no actual Transport Request number associated while transporting them.

In this blogpost we will focus on the Transportation part but however for explanation I have added brief details on the creation process too.

S/4 HANA Cloud, Public Edition

S/4 HANA Cloud, Private Edition

Creation -

Fiori Application used – Custom Fields and Logic

 

 

 

Create the new custom field for the required business context and activate it for the required areas where you want to use this custom field -

 



 

After Enabling Usage for the required areas, click on Publish for the field to be available on the selected contexts.

 

If you want to add this field to your Fiori Application’s UI then you can do that using the option Adapt UI from the particular Fiori application for which you have created and exposed the custom field.

 

 

*Same as Public Cloud in this context
Fiori Application used – Custom Fields and Logic

 

 

The steps for activating the usage of the custom field and publishing are also same in S/4 HANA Private Cloud as shown in the other column for Public Cloud so you can follow all the same screenshots given in the left column for Private Cloud environment also.

**Screenshots have not been repeated.

Transport process -

No actual Transport Request number associated. Transport of Key User Extensibility objects happens using two Fiori Applications –

 Export Software Collection (To be used in Source System)

 Import Collection (To be used in Target system)

 

Transport happens with actual Transport Request number with two Fiori applications used –

 Configure Software Packages

 Register Extensions for Transport

Transportation process in Source system -

The transportation process is done by Software Collection.

The first step is to create a Software Collection and add the Custom field in the collection. This is done by the Fiori App – Export Software Collection

 

Once you create a Software collection you have to add your Custom field or whichever Extensibility object you are planning to move by clicking on Add Items

 

 

You can filter your Objects by selecting the type of objects you want to add in the Software Collection

 

 

Once everything is added you can click on Check and then finally Export

 

First step go to the Fiori Application Configure Software Packages and add registration for your package by clicking on the button Add Registration

 

 

Add your Transport Number from the F4 search help available under the column Transport Request to assign the package to a particular Transport number available.
Optionally you can turn on the Automatic Request handling for this package with the option available.

 

 

Second step go to the Fiori Application Register Extensions For Transport

 

Select your Custom field or the Extensibility object that you want to transport and click on Reassign to Package and select the package you registered in previous step.

 

 

 

By default your Extensibility object will get assigned to the Transport to which the package is assigned but you can change the transport assignment by clicking on the option Assign to Transport Request.

You can now go to Transaction Code SE10 and check the transport number under your name. The Custom field or the  extensibility object that you added will be available in the Transport Request .

You can release and move the transport to upper environments based on the transport mechanism that is being followed in your project.

Transportation process in Target system -

Based on architecture if it is a 3-Tier Public Cloud system then the next higher environment is going to be the Test Environment in Public Cloud.

In the Test Environment open the application Import Collection

 

 

 

Select your Software collection that you exported from source system and click on Check and Import

 

 

If you want to move your Software Collection to production environment then you can move it by the Forward option in the Import Collection app

 

No such similar activity is required here. Transport will be moved and available in QA/Test environment with transport release mechanism followed.

 

**Additional information –

Use the Fiori Application Extensibility Inventory in both the S/4 HANA Cloud versions to check dependencies of objects before transporting.

entry.title: Test, Demo, and Development: Discovery Package "How‑to" Hub for SAP Cloud ERP, Private Edition
entry.content:
This blog provides a curated collection of how‑to guides, practical tips, and step‑by‑step instructions based on real requests we have received from users working with the Discovery Package for SAP Cloud ERP, Private Edition. Its purpose is to help others facing similar questions complete their tasks more efficiently. In addition, several entries highlight known limitations or issues encountered in the process—offering transparency and guidance on how to navigate them. The hub continues to grow as new questions and requests come in.

Topics covered

  • Client Copy 

  • Change ADT to logon to a different client
  • Http error 429
  • UI5 update

 

Client Copy

It is possible to run a local client copy in the Discovery package system using TA SCCLN. TA SCC_CLIENT_SIZE can be used to check on the size of a client. Due to HANA compression, this is only an approximation if more than one client is filled. The tool also supports selective size determination for specified tables.  As an example client 100 size info looks like this:  

Some useful tips: 

  • Client copies must no longer be executed from the target client. Instead, we recommend to run the Client Copy tool from a third client which is not affected by the tool. For example, when copying from client 100 to 200, you could run the tool in client 000. 

  • The client copy always locks the target client. By default, the source client is also locked. You may choose to not lock the source client at the risk of concurrent write access to the source client resulting in inconsistent copies. A client lock now not only prevents new logins using SAP GUI, but also for HTTP access. 

Here is an example of creating a new Client 101 and copying Client 100 into it. Using profile SAP_ALL copies almost all information. 

 

Change ADT to logon to a different client

If you want to logon ADT to a different client than the default client 100 (e.g. after a client copy) you can configure it as follows: 

In the https logon method, ADT uses the client specified in the profile parameter login/system_client -- client 100 in our system. This can be changed to a different client in TA RZ10 and requires a restart (see service requests in SAP for Me). Afterwards, ADT/Fiori/WebGUI will use this one as default logon client.  In Fiori and WebGui you can still specify other clients on the logon screen or by adding ?sap-client=xxx to the URL. 

This workaround cannot be used for parallel development across multiple clients. We are looking into this.

 

 

Http Error 429

When an HTTP 429 error occurs, it means the HTTP client has sent too many requests within a short period of time. 

The TDD Discovery Package system is configured with a fixed capacity of 32 CPUs and 256 GB RAM. 
If an HTTP 429 error appears, you need to reduce the number of parallel requests, for example by lowering the number of simultaneous logons. 

Please note that the TDD system is not designed for load or performance testing. 

 

UI5 Update

If you want to update the patch level of the installed SAPUI5 1.136 release in the system, proceed as follows:

1. Login with RISE_XYZ user in client 000
2. Make sure RISE_XYZ user has profile SAP_ALL assigned (check via SU01)
3. Download locally the software component (about 309MB) and the SNOTE itself from https://me.sap.com/notes/3704759/E
4. Open transaction RZ10 and load the latest version of the profile DEFAULT.PFL (Menu -> Utilities -> Import Profiles -> Of active servers)
5. Add the following line to the DEFAULT.PFL profile: icm/HTTP/max_request_size_KB = -1
6. Save and activate the profile
7. Restart the system: execute Service Request "Pre-Packaged: System Reboot" in SAP for Me
8. After the instance is back online, open transaction SNOTE
9. Click on "Upload TCI" and select the downloaded K816008CPSAPUI.SAR
10. Click on "Upload SAP Note" and select the small SAR file which was downloaded in note 3704759
11. Click on "Implement SAP Note"
12. Follow the installation prompts and generate a new transport workbench request when prompted

entry.title: Kick Off 2026 With Impact: Collaborate on 56 Fresh SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Projects
entry.content:
We’re excited to announce that Cycle 1 of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative for 2026 is now live!
This cycle features 56 new projects, offering SAP customers and partners the opportunity to gain early insights into upcoming product developments and collaborate directly with SAP teams to shape the next generation of SAP solutions.

🗓Registration is open until March 13, 2026.

In a rapidly evolving business environment, early involvement can make a meaningful difference. Through the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative, you can work directly with SAP product managers and developers, sharing your feedback on concepts, prototypes, and plans while they are still in the early stages.

To help you explore all available opportunities, we’ve prepared a complete overview of the projects.
View the full list here, or browse the links below to discover the topics most relevant to you.

SAP Ariba

  • Reporting and Analytics in SAP Ariba Invoicing

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • AI-powered Code Upgrade Automation
  • BPTX Early Insights
  • Customize SAPUI5 apps for business users - provide feedback on new and planned features
  • SAP BTP Developer's Guide
  • SAP BTP: Improving the Admin's and Solution Architect's Experience
  • SAP Integration Suite
  • Validate MCP Hub

SAP Concur

  • Intelligent Audit Evolution: Accelerating Audits with Automation and AI
  • New User Onboarding Experience in SAP Concur
  • Reimagining Travel & Expense with SAP Concur-embedded virtual cards experience
  • SAP Concur - User Profile Management
  • SAP Concur Expense: Streamlining Corporate Card Administration
  • SAP Concur: Travel and Expense Intelligence (Reporting) - Data Platform
  • SAP Concur: Travel and Expense Intelligence (Reporting) - Recommendations
  • Trip Extras - Connecting suppliers to business travelers when it matters most.
  • Verify: Shaping the Future of Auditing and Compliance

Data & Analytics

  • Exploring BDC Web & Mobile Experiences to drive user relevancy and adoption
  • Product Insights / Product 360
  • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud (XSC,XSA,CAP)

SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • A modern Output Management experience in ERP Suite
  • Agentic AI - Analytical Business Insights  
  • Agentic AI Smart Billing Assistant
  • AI Agent with Financial Correspondences
  • AI Assisted SAP Workflow Management
  • AI assisted use cases for Embedded Analytics
  • AI-assisted task automation
  • AI in Core Logistics (HUM, SN, BM, MM-IM, Kanban)
  • Business Partner and Product Master in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Concept for Harmonized Period Control
  • Configuration experience of Situation Handling in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Customer returns user experience
  • E-mails in business context with integrated business communications for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Embedded AI and Joule for S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition
  • Enhanced Payment Order Management in Automatic Payment Run Process
  • Farmer Collaboration and Engagement
  • Generate Settlement Rules
  • Identity and Access Management: Upgrade and Change Handling
  • IFRS 9- Credit-Risk Based Impairment for Trade Receivables using Advanced Valuation
  • Improving Sanction Party List screening with AI
  • Improving the Usability of Task and Application Logs in Group Reporting
  • Limit Management Modernization (Credit Risk Analyzer)
  • Material Long Texts – Automated Content Generation for Product Master
  • Parallel Unit of Measure
  • Project staffing, consider dependencies & provide smart staffing across different roles of projects
  • SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Master Data Analytics Dashboard
  • Simplify and Improve the Intrastat Declaration Process with AI
  • Task Center (One Inbox) experience evaluation in SAP Business Suite
  • Understanding Payment Reconciliation Processes and Automation Barriers in Bank Statements
  • Unlock more flexible Margin Analysis in SAP Cloud ERP

SAP Globalization

  • AI Agents for SAP Translation Hub
  • AI Assisted E-Document Error Handling : Continuous Adaptation

Others

  • Agentic AI Operations with SAP Automation Pilot
  • How Designers and Developers Use SAP Design System Resources
  • Integrated Planning in Dairy
  • Research into an Intelligent Spare Parts Request Processing Agent
  • SAP Reference Architecture

Let’s shape the future together!

Innovation happens through collaboration. By participating in the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative projects, you help SAP create better, more relevant solutions for businesses like yours. This is your chance to influence what comes next.

Don’t miss out – sign up today at influence.sap.com and be a part of the SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Cycle 1 in 2026!

Warm regards, 
Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team 

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

entry.title: Release for Billing of Billing Request Lines of an Ad Hoc Billing Plan on Service Order Item
entry.content:
In the blog Billing Plan Template in Service Order Process I have explained how you can create ad hoc billing plans on service order item with the usage of billing plan templates.

In this blog, I am going to explain how you can release billing request lines of such ad hoc billing plans for billing. As for other items, the billing document requests (BDRs) that are generated will appear in the billing due list.

First, you need to check if the service order items that have an ad hoc billing plan are Released or Completed. In addition, the entire service order must be Released at least. If this prerequisite is met, you have two options to create billing document requests (BDRs). Creation of BDRs is referred to as release for billing.

  • You can use the Release for Billing app in Fiori Launchpad or in standalone WebUI.
  • You can use the report Generate BDRs for Service Orders that you can find on the SAP Easy Access menu. Just to add, you can manually schedule jobs by using this report.

Generate Billing Document Requests using a Report

You open the SAP Easy Access menu Service -> Service Processes

You click on Generate BDRs for Service Orders. (See screenshot in Figure 1.) 

Figure 1: SAP Access Menu for Generate BDRs for Service Orders (screenshot from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025).

The report CRMS4_SERVORD_BDR_GENERATE is executed and its selection screen is opened. (See Screenshot in Figure 2.)

Figure 2: Selection Screen of Report CRMS4_SERVORD_BDR_GENERATE (screenshot from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025).

You can find the upper frame with title Service Order/Confirmation Details. You can use these selection criteria to precisely define which billing request lines should be included.

I also would like to share the great information that you can use this report also for billable service order and billable service confirmation items. I will explain which settings are required for that also in the following section.

  • BDR Creation Horizon (in Days): The BDR Creation Horizon defines how many days ahead the system should look when creating BDRs. When you run the report, it only processes billing request lines with billing dates that fall between today and your specified number of days in the future. The system automatically calculates this date range and creates BDRs for all eligible billing request lines within that timeframe.
  • Billing Date: You can define the desired billing date range for processing.
  • Service Order ID: You can specify the range of service order ID to process. This range will be considered only if you also select the Billable Service Order Items checkbox
  • Service Confirmation ID: You can specify the range of service confirmation IDs to process. This range will be considered only if you also select the Billable Confirmation Items checkbox.
  • Sold-To Party: You can specify the range of sold-to parties.
  • Sales Organization: You can specify a sales organization.
  • Distribution Channel: You can specify the range of distribution channels.
  • Division: You can specify the range of divisions.
  • Items with Billing Plans: This checkbox is selected by default. This indicates that billing request lines are included.
  • Billable Service Order Items: You can select this checkbox to include billable service order items.
  • Billable Confirmation Items: You can select this checkbox to include billable service confirmation items.

If you want to create BDRs for billing dates in the past, you can use the selection criterion Billing Date instead of BDR Creation horizon (in Days).

In the selection screen you can find the lower frame with title Processing Details. (See Screenshot in Figure 2.)

  • Simulation Only: You can select this checkbox if you want to do a test run.
  • Parallel Processing: You can select this checkbox to improve the job performance.
  • Max. Number of Parallel Tasks: You can maintain the maximum number of parallel tasks between two and five
  • Max. Number of Billable Items: You can maintain the maximum number of billable order and confirmation items for which you want to create BDRs.

For example, you want to know how many BDRs can be created for BDR creation horizon 10 days. You enter 10 as horizon, select the checkboxes for Items with Billing Plans, Billable Service Order Items, Billable Confirmation Items, and select the Simulation Only checkbox. You click on Execute button and the Display logs screen is opened. (See Screenshot in Figure 3.)

Figure 3: Display logs screen in simulation mode (screenshot from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025).

For example, you want to create BDRs immediately for certain service confirmations. You enter the IDs as range. You click on Execute button and check the outcome. (See Screenshot in Figure 4.)

Figure 4: Display logs screen (screenshot from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025).

Generate Billing Document Requests using a WebUI app

You can release billable service order, and confirmation items for billing in the Release for Billing app. (See Screenshot in Figure 5.)

Figure 5: Release for Billing app (screenshot from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025).

Starting with 2025 FPS1, billing request lines can also be released for billing in the Release for Billing app.

For this purpose, the filter criteria are enhanced. You click on Adapt Filters button. (See Screenshot in Figure 6.)

Figure 6: Adapt Filters button (screenshot from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025).

In the newly opened window Adapt Filters, you click on the drop-down list of the first filter so that the whole list is displayed. You can check that two new filters are added: BDR Creation Horizon (in Days) and Billing Date. (See Screenshot in Figure 7.) These parameters correspond to the selection fields of the report CRMS4_SERVORD_BDR_GENERATE.

Figure 7: Adapt Filters window (screenshot from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025).

In the search result list, you can check that two new columns are added, Ad Hoc Billing and Billing Date. (See Screenshot in Figure 8.)

Figure 8: Adapt Filters window (screenshot from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025).

The column Ad Hoc Billing is enabled as checkbox and indicates if this line belongs to an ad hoc billing plan.  You can use the billing date to identify the billing request line of the ad hoc billing plan of service order item.

In the example that you can see in the screenshot in figure 9, the Ad Hoc Billing checkbox for item number 10 is selected for two billing dates. That means that two billing request lines of the ad hoc billing plan on service order item 10 are found.

The third Ad Hoc Billing checkbox is not selected, so item 20 is a billable service order item.

Figure 9: Adapt Filters window (screenshot from SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2025).

Conclusion

Thank you for taking the time to read this blog! Hopefully you will have more in-depth knowledge about the possibilities to release items for billing in service order process.

Please don’t hesitate to raise your questions in Q&A forum of SAP Community or leave your comments below!

For more details, please visit the full product assistance documentation in the SAP Help Portal: Billing of Service Transactions | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: The Combined Power of SAP Joule and AI for the RISE with SAP Methodology
entry.content:
Part five of the five-part series, “RISE with SAP Methodology”

AI is everywhere right now. Not just in tech headlines or innovation labs, but also in boardrooms, architecture discussions, and project plans. And if you work with SAP solutions, you’ve likely noticed that most road maps now mention it, too.

Many of us have seen “the next big thing” come and go. New tools, new buzzwords, and big promises have often delivered limited practical impact. So, the real question isn’t whether AI exists. It’s “where will AI actually make a difference?”

Organizations want technology that helps people work better, make smarter decisions faster and cut through complexity in day-to-day work with real-world pressures and consequential deadlines. AI should not be treated as a standalone innovation topic, but as a capability embedded across products, processes and user experiences.

That’s exactly the direction SAP’s new strategy is taking. At the heart of this strategy sits a simple idea: bring applications, data, and AI together in SAP Business Suite, so intelligence becomes part of how work naturally happens, as shown in Figure 1.

Figure 1: The Future of AI in SAP Business Suite

Joule vs. AI Capabilities: Understanding the Difference

When people talk about AI in SAP environments, two concepts often get mixed together: AI capabilities and the Joule solution.

While connected, each one plays vastly distinct roles:

  • AI capabilities form the foundation and are the engine, quietly running in the background. Powerful, but often invisible, they analyze huge volumes of data, predict risks and generate recommendations. They include technologies such as machine learning, predictive analytics and automation embedded across SAP solutions.

  • Joule, on the other hand, is the interaction layer between people and AI and facilitates one-on-one conversations with the user. This is the moment when intelligence becomes accessible. Instead of searching through lots of guidelines, dashboards or compiling reports, users simply ask questions, such as “What should I focus on next in our transformation project?” or “Where are we off track?”. Users can even just say “Summarize the risks”. And in return, they get answers immediately and in context. (see Figure 2 below)

Together, AI capabilities and Joule form a complementary model where AI feels natural, not abstract. The experience is remarkably similar to talking to a knowledgeable colleague who already understands the system landscape. There’s deep intelligence behind the scenes and a simple, human interaction up front.

Figure 2: Interacting with Joule as the new AI user experience

Joule: A practical entry point to AI

As shown in Figure 2 above, one of SAP´s strategic priority is to offer a new AI user experience through Joule. This AI-enabled assistant introduces a simple, practical way to bring AI into transformation work.

While delivering many AI capabilities across the SAP solution portfolio, Joule goes beyond using AI as another analytics tool or dashboard by running an interaction layer that allows customers to work with AI through a single, conversational interface.

This changes how teams engage with information during transformation projects or in day-to-day business. Instead of navigating multiple tools, reports, or status decks, users can interact with Joule in natural language—asking questions, requesting summaries, or starting conversations on how to proceed with tasks alongside the transformation project. (see Figure 3)

Behind the scenes, Joule connects to relevant data across SAP solutions and presents answers in a way that is easy to understand and easy to act on. In practice, this lowers the barrier to insight. Instead of searching for information, teams start working interactively with it.

Figure 3: Using Joule interactively during your transformation work

Strategic enablers of the transformation journey

As mentioned before, AI is embedded directly into SAP solutions and workflows to help people navigate complexity. Users can surface relevant insights, identify patterns, and reduce manual effort.

Take, for example, transforming an ERP landscape. This project can easily become complex, depending on customer requirements. The volume of information that teams must use at the same time—including process data, system insights, project status, testing results, risks, and dependencies—often spread across tools and documents. Even with a clear methodology in place, the effort required to connect these dots manually has become a challenge of its own.

RISE with SAP Methodology already provides the structure needed by providing the integrated tooling, standardized framework, and expert guidance to support the proper execution discipline. However, embedding AI in the methodology adds a new layer of intelligence at the point of action, helping teams understand what matters where risks are emerging, and which decisions requires attention (see Figure 4).

In this case, AI reduces the load that comes with large-scale change. When applied correctly, it supports people in making better and faster decisions, especially when time and attention are limited.

Meanwhile, Joule is used whenever clarity is needed (see Figure 4). This opens up a new way of working with guidance, insights, and execution support throughout the transformation journey when using RISE with SAP Methodology, serving as an active companion.

Figure 4: Joule and AI as an integral part of RISE with SAP Methodology

Giving an end-to-end perspective alongside the transformation, Joule allows project teams to handle time-consuming and complex tasks faster and with greater ease. This includes searching for information and guidelines that can help them start and review extensive assessments and analyses.

As planning and design activities progress, Joule enables teams to stay oriented. It supports prioritization, highlights dependencies, and helps validate assumptions before decisions become difficult to reverse. AI does not replace workshops or experience, but it strengthens them with data-driven insight.

During execution, when the build and testing phases accelerate, Joule supports transparency and focus. AI capabilities detect patterns in defects, progress, and quality signals, while Joule summarizes what matters most. So instead of consolidating information manually, teams can react early.

Joule supports readiness discussions as go-live approaches. By correlating information across testing, migration, and operational checks, AI helps teams assess whether they are truly ready based on evidence, not gut feeling.

After go-live, AI continues to support continuous improvement. Usage patterns, incidents, and performance signals are analyzed in the background. Joule helps connect teams to those insights to understand where to improve next and how operational issues relate back to earlier transformation decisions.

Practical value over automation

The strength of AI in SAP transformations does not lie in automation alone.
It lies in reducing all the cognitive load that happens during the transformation project.

By combining all elements of the RISE with SAP Methodology:

  • a clear path (with the Standardized Framework)
  • Integrated tooling (with the Integrated toolchain)
  • End-to-end Execution support (with the Expert Guidance) and
  • AI-driven assistance through Joule and specific AI capabilities

customers are well equipped to move through their transformation with focus, confidence and consistency.

AI does not replace people or experience.
It supports both – at the right moment, in the right context.

To learn more about RISE with SAP Methodology, read our in-depth overview.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

RISE with SAP Methodology - Blog-series 

Discover the 5-part blog series:

  • Blog 1/5 (December 3rd, 2025)
    RISE with SAP Methodology: A practical Guide to Modernizing with Confidence
  • Blog 2/5 (December 10th, 2025)
    Deep Dive: Inside the framework of RISE with SAP Methodology
  • Blog 3/5 (January 14th, 2026)
    The Integrated Toolchain in a Nutshell
  • Blog 4/5 (January 28th, 2026)
    Expert Guidance: Your Guided Transformation Accelerator
  • Blog 5/5 (February 11th, 2026)
    The Combined Power of SAP Joule and AI for the RISE with SAP Methodology

 --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#clouderpprivate #s4hanacloud #sap #risewithsap #transformation #risewithsapmethodology

entry.title: The Top 5 Things You Always Wanted to Know About Situation Handling
entry.content:
Situation Handling let’s you alert appropriate users about critical business issues requiring their attention. If you're curious about how it works or need more details, this blog post is for you. Additionally, we've enhanced our documentation - be sure to take a look here: Documentation.

1)    How does it really work?

Situation Handling detects business issues in your systems. The detection works for issues with either business objects or error and warning messages. In the following, these will simply be referred to as “objects”. An issue is typically defined by specific conditions or events happening that are assigned to the objects.  – and then, voilà, either a routine daily batch run or business event monitoring identifies the objects with issues.* It can be, for example, an expiring sales contract, an overbooked flight, and many more. This leads to the next question:

2)    How do I specify the business issues to be addressed by Situation Handling? How is the setup done?

Let’s understand this first: Situation Handling can detect issues from only one object or between two associated objects. If only one object is involved, that’s quite easy – the object triggering an issue, with certain conditions or events, is the very same object that helps to solve it by changing itself in a specific app. One example would be a sales quotation that has expired and needs to be checked for purchase details in order to get back to the customers.

How two associated objects work: One is usually triggering the situation. For example, the situation is triggered because the object is newly created, extends a certain value, or expires. The other object is the one that needs to be changed because it’s the one affected. This is also called the anchor object, as it “anchors” the situation. For example, a change in the processor may cause a purchasing document item to require a review. Alternatively, missing supplier confirmations make operational purchasers check the schedule lines of purchase order items again.

For the Situation Handling engine to detect situations, it needs to know what to look for. The objects with their conditions, events, and possible mappings between them need to be defined. Your business area may have very specific business issues – we got you covered with a wide range of ready-to-use situation templates. They are called “templates” because elements (like conditions) are predefined, while others (like condition filters or your specific situation texts) can be adapted by you. Just copy a template into your personal situation type and enable it.

I can already anticipate your next question: What if my case is not on the list? No problem at all – with our new framework, you can define all parts of Situation Handling yourself. It’s called “extended framework” because of its extended functionalities.

3)    And how do I alert someone? This seems complicated.

I’m not gonna lie, it seems complicated. It’s seems complicated at first, because we’ve provided all the different ways to alert someone that we (and you!) could possibly think of. First, your company’s organizational structure – a lot of customers want to alert specific team members based on their job functions. But we also offer the possibility to alert people depending on the data a situation provides – for example, the creator of a sales order, its processor, or other field values. For more information, check out the documentation.

4)    Let me try it 🤩

Now you must be wondering: Can I check this out? Sure – all SAP S/4HANA, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition customers have Situation Handling included. Just assign the “Business Process Specialist” role to your user and open the “Manage Situation Types” app. Our situation template “Sales Order Is Close to Expiry” is auto-activated, which means you can immediately check it out under “Situation Types”.

If you want to try setting up something very simple, we recommend following the test script from SAP Best Practices of our Situation Handling scope item (31N)  under “Accelerators”. If you’re more interested in our extended framework, we’ve even built three demo situation templates and our own demo app for creating dummy data. Check it out in this blog post.

5)    Uff… I have issues (and I don’t mean my business issues 😫). I did all the setup, and Situation Handling doesn’t seem to work. No one gets alerted.

No worries at all. There’s already a very well-written blog post that provides a lot of guidance.

6)    What next?

Interested? Great. We have a lot more to offer.

  • Let’s get started 😊 Check out all situation templates to see if any fit your business issues. For the setup, check out Getting Started.
  • Already a pro? Check out how you can improve your Situation Handling by monitoring it and changing certain parameters in this blog post.
  • Interested in AI? Stay tuned 😎
  • Need more help with Responsibility Management and alerting relevant users? There’s also a blog post series available.
  • Start creating your custom use cases (if you haven’t done so already): Blog post

Last but not least: I’m one of your technical writers for Situation Handling. Not only do I provide you with six steps when you only expected five, I’m also always happy to help 🫡This is our documentation (quick link: http://help.sap.com/s4_ce_situationhandling for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition and http://help.sap.com/s4_op_situationhandling for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition/SAP S/4HANA) and we love to hear your feedback.

Don’t forget to comment on anything you didn’t get, missed, would have loved to explore further – I promise, we will react! And of course, comment on all the things you liked – then we know what to keep. Thank you 🫶

More questions? Comment on this blog post, and we will try to answer them 😊

@Christiane_Poreski 
@angelika_salmen 
@sebastian_doeweling 
@axel_spriestersbach 
@Dr_Joerg_Rett 
@Vidya_Shetty1 
@NisargaPuttegowda 

_____

* For sure, monitoring business events is way more elegant than a batch run, as it catches issues right away (with some delay due to system load though, but still fairly recent). But obviously, not all conditions can be detected through events, so a once-a-day batch run is performed on the objects to identify those with issues. A batch run also has its advantages, for example, it sends controlled information once a day with no spam by frequent events.

entry.title: Introducing ACM with Advanced CPE (Commodity Pricing Engine)
entry.content:
Introduction

Agricultural Contract Management (ACM) relies on the Commodity Pricing Engine (CPE) to precisely calculate commodity‑related condition types. A key element of agricultural pricing is the handling of Quantity based pricing for both future and basis components—where the future reflects market‑driven commodity prices, and the basis represents logistics‑related costs such as transportation distance.

 
As business operations expanded, the existing CPE platform struggled to efficiently handle the growing volume of pricing lots. To address these limitations and deliver significantly improved performance, CPE solution has been re‑engineered with the introduction of the Advanced Commodity Pricing Engine (Advanced‑CPE). This modern architecture features a simplified data model, RAP‑based business objects, enhanced APIs, and redesigned interfaces—enabling high‑volume vehicle price lot creation and supporting more complex pricing scenarios with greater speed, and flexibility. More details on Advanced CPE can be found in SAP Note 3628704
 

Key Highlights in ACM with Advanced‑CPE

Use of new CPE Data Model for Pricing: The ACM solution has adopted RAP‑based redesigned commodity pricing data structures, enabling simpler and faster processing of high‑volume price lots. API and Fiori channels utilize the new CPE BO interface to efficiently execute pricing and related operations, while in the SAP GUI for ACM contracts, CPE pricing is now triggered through the RAP Business Object using EML. 

Integrated CPE Improvements in Fiori Applications: The adoption of a modernized CPE data model in ACM—supported by updated CDS views, OData services, and newly developed Fiori applications—significantly streamlines and enhances contract creation, modification, price lot processing, and approval in alignment with the redesigned CPE logic and data structures.
On the GUI side, compatible updates were introduced via new ACM Pricing Screen 4943.

Manual Addition of Secondary Basis: This new capability is available exclusively in the ACM Trading Contract app and addresses a key business need for greater flexibility in Agricultral commodity price management. It allows users to manually add a secondary basis condition(s) to the existing pricing conditions that were originally determined through standard pricing procedures. This manual secondary basis can be introduced at any stage—whether during the creation of a new fixation or even after a fixation has already been completed for a price lot.

Streamlined Pricing Approaches - The Componentized and Componentized with Secondary Basis pricing approaches have been merged into a single, unified Componentized pricing approach. Other pricing approaches - Componentized Flat, Adjustable Flat, and Non‑Hedgeable—remain available. More detail are avaialble here ACM Pricing Approach  

Control of Maturity Key Date Based on Business Requirements -  The new Maturity Selection Routine automatically identifies valid maturity key dates that occur after the reference calculation date and current date. It checks each date against the active trading status in table /ACCGO/T_MATKEYD and selects the earliest date that is still actively traded. For more information, please refer to the updated documentation here: Maturity Selection Routine

Introduction of New ACM Routines for CPE - ACM has customized and adopted the following routines:

  1. 19001 - ACM Maturity Selection (Maturity Selection Routine)
  2. 19013 - ACM Last Day of the Month (Date Offset Routines).
  3. 19001 - Start of Commodity Item Delivery Period (Reference Date Routines)
  4. 19002 - Midpoint of Delivery Period (Reference Date Routines)
  5. 19003 - End of Commodity Item Delivery Period (Reference Date Routines)
  6. 19004 - Midpoint of CI Delivery Period + Payment Terms (Reference Date Routines)

For more information, please refer to the updated documentation here: CPE Routines in ACM

Price Lot Quantity Un-changed on Cancellation - In ACM with Advanced CPE, the behavior of cancellation on a price lot has been changed now. Previously, cancelling quantity on a price lot would directly reduce the lot’s quantity. In the new solution, the price‑lot quantity remains unchanged but cancellation records are created, like before, to represent the cancelled quantity for that lot.

Data Model Simplification for Cancellation: The legacy tables /ACCGO/T_CANC_CC and /ACCGO/T_CANC_PL, previously used for storing cancellation data in ACM, are now obsolete in the Advanced CPE solution.
A new consolidated table, /ACCGO/T_CC_CANC, has been introduced to store all cancellation information, and the table /ACCGO/T_COMSUB, which captured cancelled quantities at contract and item level in ACM, has also been deprecated now.

Use of New CPE Configuration for Secondary Basis or Non‑Hedgeable Conditions Without Market Data: Advanced CPE now supports Term Calculation Type 40 (Formula Rate Leading without Market Data), enabling formula‑rate conversion without accessing market data. When this new type is selected, the ‘Term Rate Type’ field becomes available, allowing configuration of whether the term condition refers to a Secondary Basis or a Non‑Hedgeable condition. For details on other Term Calculation Types in CPE, please refer to the updated documentation: Term Calculation Type in Advanced Commodity Pricing Engine .  

Display of Floating Prices in Fiori Application: When prices or exchange rates are not fixed for all conditions in a price lot, the Fiori apps display the Provisional Total, Provisional Cumulative Basis, and Provisional Future Price. This feature is available in both the Manage ACM Contract and Process ACM Price Lots apps, and further details are provided in the “Display of Floating Prices” section of the updated documentation:  Floating Prices in Manage ACM Contract App ,  Floating Prices in Process ACM Price Lots App . This feature was released in 2025 FPS01 and is also available in 2025 FPS0 via an SAP Note.

Filter Price Lots by Consumption: A new filter criterion is added to the Process ACM Price Lots app to filter the price lots based on Assigned/Un-assigned Quantity. More details can be found in the updated documentation: Filter Price Lots by Consumption . This feature was released in 2025 FPS01 and is also available in 2025 FPS0 via an SAP Note. 

Selection for the Number of Records Based on Threshold Value: The system limits how many records can be processed at once for mass actions in the Process ACM Price Lots app. If your selection exceeds this system‑defined threshold, you’ll be prompted to refine the filters to reduce the number of records. Only the records within this limit will be processed for the chosen mass action. More details can be found in the updated documentation: Selection for the Number of Records Based on Threshold Value . This feature was released in 2025 FPS01 and is also available in 2025 FPS0 via an SAP Note. 

Enhanced the Interface for Contract Change API : The Contract Change API (/ACCGO/CCAK_CHANGE_API) has been upgraded to extend its capabilities beyond contract changes. It now fully supports pricing-related operations, including Lift, Roll, Cancellation, and Closure. Previously, these pricing actions were not accessible through this API. With this enhancement, the API now serves as a single unified access point for managing a broad range of contract and pricing operations, effectively closing the functional gap that existed earlier.

 
Elimination of Un‑Priced Lot Storage in ACM: The database table /ACCGO/T_NPELOTS, previously used to store temporary lot ($tmp) quantities, has been made obsolete in the Advanced CPE solution. In Advanced CPE, ACM is no longer required to store un‑priced quantities. This information is now fully maintained by CPE, which captures the un‑priced quantity at the contract and item level within its own database tables.

Streamlining CDOTE Handling in Advanced CPE: The database table /ACCGO/CDOTE_BUF, previously used in ACM to store CDOTE‑related order information, has been deprecated in the Advanced CPE solution. In Advanced CPE, CDOTE processing is fully handled by CPE, eliminating the need for ACM to store this data. ACM will now only forward CDOTE‑related information to CPE—either through the CPE BO interface or via the RAP BO. Once received, CPE will process the data internally and trigger the CDOTE API from its side. 

Availability of ACM with Advanced‑CPE and Activation Overview

 
In the SAP S/4HANA 2025 FPS0 release, ACM is available in two deployment variants:
  1. ACM with the traditional (classic) CPE
  2. ACM with the new Advanced‑CPE
Both existing and new ACM customers can choose between classic CPE and Advanced‑CPE. Existing customers with the /ACCGO/SWITCH_ECC switch continue to be supported on both options, while new customers also have access to both. However, for all implementations, Advanced‑CPE is the recommended approach, offering  enhanced performance and pricing capabilities.

The Advanced‑CPE functionality is not enabled by default. Both ACM and CPE have delivered the new capabilities behind dedicated business switche or switch based feature toggle which must be activated before the Advanced‑CPE–based processing can be used. This controlled activation ensures flexibility and smooth adoption for all customers.

 
For new ACM customers, Activate Advanced‑CPE by turning on the feature toggle S4OP_FTGL_CMM_CPE_REDESIGN using the Manage Feature Toggles (FTM) Fiori app. After this, enable ACM adoption by activating the switch /ACCGO/NEXTGEN_CPE_ADOPTION (business function: /ACCGO/NEXTGEN_CPE_ADOPTION). This completes ACM’s readiness to work with the redesigned Advanced‑CPE architecture.
 
For Existing ACM customers, switch activation requires an additional prerequisite: all CPE data conversion steps must be completed successfully before enabling the new CPE version. Once Advanced‑CPE is activated, reverting to the classic CPE is not possible. Detailed guidance on switch activation, data conversion, and ACM adoption of Advanced‑CPE is provided in SAP Note 3631220.
 

Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)

 
1. What is this new solution about?
The solution introduces ACM with Advanced‑CPE, designed to modernize and simplify pricing in ACM while delivering enhanced performance and new capabilities.
 
2. Which SAP Release and Versions support ACM with Adoption CPE Solution?
This solution is available with SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition (PCE) 2025 FPS0.
 
3. If I am existing ACM customer and want to upgrade to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition 2025 FPS0 then it is mandatory to use ACM with Advanced CPE ?
It is recommended, though not mandatory, as it offers a modern and flexible pricing architecture.
 
4. If I am existing ACM customer and willing to use ACM with Advanced CPE then how to use it ?
You must first upgrade to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition 2025 FPS0 and complete CPE data migration and then perform the switch activation. The full procedure is documented in SAP Note 3631220 .
 
5. Data Migration is required for new ACM customers too if they upgrade to SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition 2025 FPS0 and want to use ACM with Advanced CPE?
New customers only need to activate the required ACM and CPE switches. The full procedure is documented in SAP Note 3631220 .
 
6. Can i activate ACM and CPE switches in any order for this new solution?
The CPE switch must be activated first, as the ACM switch depends on it.
 
7. What are the new apps for ACM with Advanced-CPE?
New Fiori apps have been introduced for Contract & Pricing:
  • Manage ACM Contracts (App ID: F5198A) - Refer to the updated documentation here - Contract App
  • Process ACM Price Lots (App ID: F5328A) -  Refer to the updated documentation here - Pricing App 
  • Approve ACM Contracts (App ID: F4379A) - Refer to the updated documentation here - Approval App 
8. Do i need to assign new Business role to my user for access new apps?
The existing ACM business role SAP_BR_MASTER_DATA_ACM is valid for both old and new apps.
 
9. After introducing new apps in 2025 FPS0 for ACM Contract & Pricing, old apps are obsolete?
The new apps will operate only when the Advanced‑CPE switch is enabled; otherwise, the old apps will remain in use. 

 10. How can I share feedback or ask more questions?

You can comment under this blog or post in the SAP Community Q&A with the tag ACM with Advanced CPE.
